petitioner_background: The petitioner sought classification as an employment-based immigrant under section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act as an alien of extraordinary ability in athletics. No further details about the petitioner's specific field, nationality, or career qualifications are provided in the decision text.
outcome_reasoning: The appeal was dismissed as moot. Subsequent to filing the instant petition, the petitioner filed a Form I-485 Application to Adjust Status based on a family-based petition, which USCIS approved on June 1, 2012. Because the alien had adjusted to lawful permanent resident status, further pursuit of the extraordinary ability petition was moot. The AAO did not reach the merits of whether the petitioner had established sustained national or international acclaim.
legal_citations: Section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act
fraud_or_procedural_issues: The appeal was dismissed on procedural/mootness grounds rather than on the merits. The petitioner adjusted to lawful permanent resident status through a family-based petition (Form I-485 approved June 1, 2012) while the appeal was pending, rendering the extraordinary ability petition moot.
